title: The Twenty-Fourth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## November 15, 2020 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Twenty-Fourth Sunday after Pentecost, falling within Ordinary Time and exactly two weeks before the First Sunday of Advent (November 29, 2020). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have included Christ the King Sunday themes with Matthew 25:31-46 (the judgment of nations) as the Gospel, paired with Ezekiel 34:11-16 or similar passages emphasizing Christ's role as shepherd and judge. The season carries eschatological weight—the church year's final Sundays before Advent always emphasize Christ's reign and the end times, which would have resonated distinctly in 2020's particular cultural moment.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of its 79th General Convention (held virtually in July 2020 due to COVID-19), which had passed several significant resolutions on racial justice and climate action in response to summer 2020 social movements. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Lucas (elected in 2018), was engaged in ongoing pandemic response protocols and racial reconciliation work. Saint John's Cathedral itself, as the seat of the diocesan bishop, would have been central to the diocese's messaging around these national church priorities during a particularly fraught election season.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the immediate aftermath of the November 3 presidential election, with vote-counting still underway nationally and significant local political attention on Colorado's results (the state had voted Democratic). The city was experiencing the second wave of COVID-19 cases, with Colorado hitting record hospitalizations in mid-November. Weather was typical for Denver in mid-November—cool mornings with highs around 50°F, clear skies. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where the Cathedral sits, had seen increased homeless encampments and service demands throughout the pandemic.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS

The top stories dominating American consciousness were: (1) the contested 2020 presidential election, with major networks calling Pennsylvania for Joe Biden on November 7, effectively determining the outcome, and President Trump beginning to contest results; (2) COVID-19 surging toward its winter peak, with the U.S. hitting 250,000+ deaths and hospitals increasingly strained; (3) the ongoing Black Lives Matter movement following the summer's protests, with continued police violence cases under scrutiny. The election's aftermath was creating deep national division and uncertainty about the peaceful transfer of power.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The leading international stories included: (1) the U.S. election itself being watched globally with concern about democratic stability and international standing; (2) the COVID-19 pandemic ravaging Europe with new lockdowns in France, UK, and Germany in response to exponential case growth; (3) tensions in the Middle East with Iran's Qassem Soleimani's anniversary (killed January 2020) being marked amid continued U.S.-Iran escalation risks. The pandemic was creating humanitarian crises across developing nations with minimal vaccine access.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was experiencing profound collective anxiety and polarization. The election's outcome remained uncertain to many due to Trump's refusal to concede, creating a destabilized sense of democratic norms and institutional trust. There was pandemic fatigue and grief—death tolls were rising, isolation continued, and holiday seasons loomed without their normal gatherings. Culturally, there was hunger for meaning-making and spiritual grounding; churches specifically were wrestling with how to address political division and offer prophetic witness. This sermon would have been preached into a congregation carrying election trauma, grief, and questions about hope and God's ultimate justice—making Matthew 25's judgment narrative particularly charged and relevant.
